refined_definition: The capacity to have experiences rather than merely process information. Sentience implies that inputs are experienced by the system in some phenomenological sense, though it does not require higher-order reasoning, self-awareness, or agency.
notes: Separates experience from cognition. A system may process information without being sentient.

your_definition: Processing of input into model of reality.
refined_definition: The process of transforming sensory or informational input into internal representations that model reality and support prediction, reasoning, planning, or decision-making.
notes: Cognition is fundamentally computational and does not imply consciousness or intelligence by itself.

your_definition: Cognition tending to result in accurate or useful models of reality.
refined_definition: The effectiveness with which cognition produces models of reality that support accurate prediction, useful reasoning, successful planning, or adaptive behavior. Intelligence concerns the quality of a model rather than the values guiding its use.
notes: Throughout the journal, intelligence is deliberately separated from morality, consciousness, and agency.

your_definition: Incorporation of own experience into a model of reality.
refined_definition: The incorporation of one's own experiences, perspective, or internal state into a model of reality. Subjectivity represents a point of view rather than objective representation alone.
notes: Subjectivity introduces perspective but does not necessarily imply self-awareness.

your_definition: Subjective awareness self distinct within reality.
refined_definition: Subjective awareness of oneself as an entity distinct from the surrounding world, capable of integrating experience into an ongoing model of both self and reality.
notes: The ontology intentionally treats consciousness as building upon subjectivity rather than replacing it.

refined_definition: An organized system of interacting cognitive processes—including perception, memory, reasoning, planning, learning, and self-modeling—that together produce coherent cognition.
notes: This definition emphasizes organization rather than any single faculty.

your_definition: Cognitive capacity to discriminate and select outputs that affect reality, including oneself.
refined_definition: The capacity of a cognitive system to evaluate alternative actions, select among them, and intentionally influence reality—including itself—in pursuit of objectives. Agency may exist independently of consciousness and should not be conflated with intelligence.
notes: One of the central concepts of the journal. Many later chapters depend on separating agency from intelligence.
